front: Author: murders of hundreds of women in the fictional city of Santa Teresa, based on Ciudad Juarez femicides
Author: Ulises Lima and Arturo Belano search for Cesarea Tinajero, the mysterious founder of Visceral Realism
Author: reclusive German author Benno von Archimboldi is revealed to actually be Hans Reiter
Author: journalist Oscar Fate is sent to cover a boxing match in Santa Teresa
Author: five-part novel with sections titled 'The Part about the Critics,' 'The Part about Amalfitano,' 'The Part about Fate,' etc.
Author: four literary critics (Pelletier, Morini, Espinoza, Norton) search for Archimboldi in Santa Teresa
Author: narrator Auxilio Lacouture hides in a bathroom stall for thirteen days during a fictionalization of the Tlatelolco Massacre
Author: fictional encyclopedia of Nazi-affiliated authors who escaped to South America after WWII
back: Roberto Bolano (Nazi Literature in the Americas)

front: Author: Amalfitano is a professor who goes mad, makes geometric drawings with philosopher names, and hangs Euclid's Elements from a clothesline
Author: Father Urrutia recounts educating Pinochet and his generals about Marxism
Author: fictionalized himself as Arturo Belano and Mario Santiago Papasquiaro as Ulises Lima
Author: road trip in a Ford Impala to the Sonora Desert with the prostitute Lupe to find a visceral realist poet
Author: Hans Reiter as a child had a one-legged father and one-eyed mother, stole Animals and Plants of the European Coastal Region, became obsessed with seaweed
Author: ex-Black Panther Barry Seaman wrote the cookbook 'Eating Ribs with Barry Seaman'
Author: poetry collection The Romantic Dogs includes the poem 'Godzilla in Mexico'
Author: condemned Isabel Allende's work as ranging 'from kitsch to the pathetic'
Author: a novel consisting of a single sentence about the experiences of a heroin addict named Beach
Author: Archimboldi's novels include The Garden, The Leather Mask, D'Arsonval, and Bifurcaria Bifurcata
Author: novel opens with a Baudelaire epigraph about 'an oasis of horror in a desert of boredom'
Author: a church desecrator known as 'the Penitent' who urinates in churches with gallons of urine
